Introduction.script: The paper proposes the Transformer as an attention-based architecture for sequence transduction without relying on recurrence or convolution.
bullet_points: It is introduced mainly in the context of machine translation.
The design is motivated by better parallel training and easier long-range dependency modeling.

Methodology.script: The model uses stacked encoder and decoder layers built from multi-head attention, feed-forward networks, residual connections, and layer normalization. Positional encodings are added because the model has no recurrence.
bullet_points: Scaled dot-product attention computes weights using queries, keys, and values.
Masked self-attention in the decoder blocks access to future target tokens.
Multiple attention heads learn different relations in parallel.

Results.script: The Transformer delivers strong translation results on WMT 2014 English-to-German and English-to-French benchmarks while also reducing training cost compared with earlier recurrent systems.
bullet_points: The base model reports 27.3 BLEU on English-to-German.
The big model reports 28.4 BLEU on English-to-German.
The big model reports 41.8 BLEU on English-to-French.

Discussion.script: The paper argues that self-attention shortens the path between tokens and makes optimization more hardware-friendly because all positions can be processed in parallel.
bullet_points: Attention heads exhibit different learned behaviors.
The model is presented as a general sequence modeling architecture, not only a translation system.

Conclusion.script: The work shows that attention alone can be enough for high-quality sequence modeling and establishes the Transformer as a strong alternative to recurrent models.
bullet_points: The paper becomes a foundation for many later NLP systems.
